commits.subject: fix(edges): clamp storage bar + empty-state when all rows removed from review
commits.body:
Two edge cases from `edges branch`.

1. Storage progress bar/aria-label could read >100%.
   The WHATWG StorageManager spec allows `usage > quota` briefly — the
   browser can shrink an origin's quota (user clears site data, domain
   rebalancing between origins). Without a clamp, aria-label reads
   "Storage 105% used" until the next loadStorageUsage() catches up.
   Added Math.min(100, …).

2. ImportStepReview rendered blank when user removed every row.
   The new X-button delete flow (commit 561673a) lets users splice any
   row out of the review list. Removing all rows left a blank area
   with a disabled "Import 0 transactions" button and no path forward.
   Added an empty-state card with a "Go back to upload" button wired
   to the existing emit('back') contract.

Dropped from the audit:
- "CashflowGraph downloads for empty workspaces" — verified wrong.
  The chart is inside `<template v-else>` gated on
  `transactions.length > 0`; empty-state workspaces correctly skip
  the ApexCharts chunk download.
- "Tag-suggestion spinner is perpetual" — useTagSuggestions has a
  10s INFERENCE_TIMEOUT that resolves []; no infinite spin possible.
- "0% bar when quota is 0" — the `quota > 0` guard at
  loadStorageUsage prevents storageUsage being set, so the indicator
  is hidden entirely in that case.

Type-check clean. 145/145 tests pass. Production build succeeds.

commits.subject: fix(race): sync-flush dark-mode watchers to honor syncing flag
commits.body:
Cross-tab dark-mode sync had a stale-flag race. handleStorageSync
sets syncing=true, mutates isDark + currentComboId, sets
syncing=false, all synchronously — then calls applyTheme(..., true).

Vue's default watch scheduling is post-flush: the isDark / combo
watchers fire on the next microtask. By then syncing is already
false, the `if (syncing) return` guard passes, and both watchers
run applyTheme with skipPersist=false + emit debug log entries.
Net: 3 applyTheme calls + 2 redundant localStorage writes +
2 duplicated debug entries per cross-tab sync event.

No ping-pong loop (localStorage setItem only fires storage events
in OTHER tabs when the value actually changes; the values match
what was just synced). Not corruption, just wasted work the
syncing flag was designed to prevent.

Fix: `{ flush: 'sync' }` on both watchers so they run synchronously
on ref mutation, while the syncing flag is still true. Single
applyTheme call per sync event; single localStorage write; single
debug entry.

Other findings from the race sweep were false alarms:
- Debounce timers on unmount: onUnmounted already clearTimeouts both
  cashSaveTimeout and runwayDebounceTimer.
- ML request on disposed worker: dispose() rejects pending requests.
- Radiogroup rapid keys: focus order is correct even with overlapping
  nextTick awaits (last-wins matches last-keypress).
- Print-theme fallback cancel: dataset.printTheme saved before swap,
  so cancel restores correctly.
- ConfirmDialog double-click: parent guard (`if (restoring.value) return`)
  handles it synchronously before second emit's handler runs.

Type-check clean. 145/145 tests pass.

Tags: race, dark-mode, vue-reactivity, flush-sync
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 1
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: ux-polish

commits.subject: fix(errors): clear pendingImportData on replace error + log cash-on-hand write failures
commits.body:
Two real error-handling gaps from `errors branch`.

1. useRestoreWorkspace.confirmReplace: catch branch previously left
   `pendingImportData` populated after a failed import. A subsequent
   file pick would show the confirm dialog with the OLD file's
   workspace name until the new file's validation overwrote it. Now
   cleared in the catch alongside showReplaceConfirm.

2. WorkspaceDashboard cashOnHand watcher previously had a silent
   catch marked "non-critical persistence". The value IS the user's
   entered financial data (not a UI pref); silent failure meant
   alpha reports would never surface DB-write issues on this path.
   Switched to debugLog('db', 'error', ...) so the failure shows in
   debug reports without disturbing the user (the value is still in
   the input ref — UI remains usable).

Dropped from the audit:
- CashflowGraph `runway.endBalance` optional-chain: type is
  non-optional; adding defensive chains defends against impossible
  state per CLAUDE.md "don't add error handling for scenarios that
  can't happen."
- useDarkMode storage-sync try/catch: agent itself flagged as
  low-risk; string comparisons are safe.

Deferred to TODO:
- TransactionEditModal save fire-and-forget: modal closes before
  DB write resolves; error surfaces on the dashboard rather than
  in context. Fixing requires restructuring the emit-based flow
  (child-waits-for-parent via callback or promise). Not
  data-loss — reopening shows the unsaved state and a dashboard
  banner appears — but feedback is loosely coupled.

Type-check clean. 145/145 tests pass.

commits.subject: i18n(chart): browser-locale axis + tooltip dates in CashflowGraph
commits.body:
Replaces ApexCharts' built-in format strings ('dd MMM' and
'dd MMM yyyy') with custom formatters backed by
toLocaleDateString(undefined, …). ApexCharts' internal format string
always emits English month abbreviations regardless of browser
locale; a custom formatter defers to the OS/browser preference.

Matches the formatting scheme already used elsewhere:
- useFormat.formatDateForDisplay uses toLocaleDateString(undefined, …)
- CashflowGraph tooltip Y values + SR chart summary use
  toLocaleString(undefined, …)
So the chart axes are now the last place picking up the locale.

Effect today: zero visible change — the entire UI is still English,
so English month abbreviations were consistent. The value is
forward-looking: the moment any user-facing copy is translated,
the chart axes track the locale automatically without a separate
change.

section).

commits.subject: perf(dashboard): debounce cashOnHand for runway recompute
commits.body:
Typing in the Cash on hand input previously fired a keystroke-level
reactivity cascade: cashOnHand → runway computed → CashflowGraph
series prop → ApexCharts cash-balance series re-render. While
individual steps are fast (runway walks ~90-365 daily points;
ApexCharts updates series in place), the per-keystroke visible
flicker in the chart is jarring mid-type.

Added a 300ms debounced mirror `cashOnHandForRunway` wired via
watch(cashOnHand, …) → setTimeout. Runway reads the debounced ref
for both its gating check and the calculation. Timer is cleared on
unmount alongside the existing 500ms DB-save timer.

DB persistence path is unchanged (still debounced 500ms). The new
300ms affects only the reactive recompute → chart redraw.

Type-check clean. 116/116 tests pass.

commits.subject: feat: storage-usage indicator, code-split ApexCharts, delete in review
commits.body:
Three S-sized feature items from docs/TODO.md.

1. Storage usage indicator on workspace list.
   Small footer on WorkspaceListView showing quota usage via
   navigator.storage.estimate() — icon + "N MB of M MB used" + progress
   bar. Hidden when the API isn't supported (older Safari / some Android)
   or while the list is still loading. Read once on mount; pull-to-refresh
   re-triggers loadWorkspaces but storage itself stays static — users can
   hard-reload for a fresh estimate if they just imported a large batch.

2. Code-split CashflowGraph / ApexCharts.
   CashflowGraph is the sole consumer of the ~500 KB ApexCharts bundle.
   Wrapped in defineAsyncComponent so ApexCharts loads on demand instead
   of on initial page paint. Workspace list, import wizard, and settings
   pages no longer download the chart library.

   Bundle impact (verified in production build):
     WorkspaceDetailView chunk: 560 KB → 40 KB
     CashflowGraph chunk (new, async): 523 KB (+ apexcharts 517 KB shared)

   Loading fallback is a chart-sized inline skeleton (matches breakpoint
   heights 280/350/420) with role="status" + aria-label so AT users hear
   "Loading chart…" instead of silence. 100ms delay prevents a flash
   when the chunk is cached.

3. Remove-row affordance in import review.
   ImportStepReview previously had only "Ignore" which dims a row and
   excludes it from import but keeps it on screen. Added a trash X
   button per row that splices the transaction out of the review list
   entirely — for users who've marked a bunch as ignored and want the
   screen quieter. tagInputIndex is reconciled on splice so a mid-edit
   index on a later row still points at the same row. 28×28 focus-ring
   touch target, aria-label includes the description.

Type-check clean. 116/116 tests pass. Production build succeeds.

   onMounted); `typeof document === 'undefined'` can't fire. Kept

   matchMedia change listener + onMounted/onUnmounted wiring down

   toggles are vanishingly rare; users can reload to pick up a

   than it buys. Also removed `reducedMotion` from the chart :key

commits.subject: fix(dark-mode): replace invalid 'black' print-theme fallback with 'night'
commits.body:
AppLayout's onAfterPrint restored data-theme via
`dataset.printTheme || 'black'`. 'black' is not among the themes
registered in src/index.css (cmyk, night) — if onAfterPrint ever
fires without a matching onBeforePrint (or if printTheme stored an
empty string because data-theme was absent at print time), DaisyUI
silently falls back to its default theme instead of the user's
original dark theme. Replaced with 'night' — the correct dark-theme
fallback, reachable only when printWasDark was 'true'.

Narrow edge case but easy to get right. Single-line change.

Other findings from the dark-mode sweep were either intentional
design (pre-framework inline debug pill renders dark by design —
per glow-props DEBUG_SYSTEM pattern; the diagnostic surface must
be CSS-independent and visually distinct from app theme), require
browser-level contrast measurement rather than code changes, or
the agent itself flagged as "no action needed".

Type-check clean. 116/116 tests pass. Production build succeeds.

commits.subject: fix(copy): remove accounting jargon and clarify ambiguous labels
commits.body:
High-severity
1. "Import Actuals" → "Import transactions".
   "Actuals" is bookkeeping jargon non-technical users don't recognise.
   Fixed in ImportStepUpload.vue h1 and WorkspaceDetailView.vue import
   button title. The route name `import-actuals` is internal (URL path
   is /workspace/:id/import — clean) so left unchanged.
   Also caught by verification: CashflowGraph chart legend showed
   "Actuals" as the series name — changed to "Spending" (+ updated
   the colour-match switch and the screen-reader summary string).

Medium-severity
2. "Continue to classify" → "Review & import".
   Next step title is "Review transactions" — old copy told users they
   were going somewhere else ("classify"). New copy matches the wizard
   step labels + states the final action in the button.
3. Import save-error clarified.
   "Couldn't save imported data. Please try again." →
   "Couldn't save your transactions. Your review is still loaded —
    please try again." The added clause tells the user their work
   isn't lost on retry, which the old message left ambiguous.
4. "Mark all unmatched as once-off" → "Mark remaining as one-time".
   "Unmatched" leaked an implementation detail (didn't match a
   pattern). "Remaining" + "one-time" matches the existing user
   vocabulary in the classification badges.
5. Pattern-matching dev-speak swept from ImportStepReview progress UI.
   "Preparing tag suggestions and pattern matching..." →
   "Preparing tag suggestions and recurring detection…"
   Progress bar label "Pattern matching" → "Recurring detection".
   Error states updated consistently ("Recurring detection unavailable",
   "Smart detection unavailable — you can still review manually").

Dropped finding: agent flagged ErrorBoundary's "Something went wrong"
as inconsistent with the "Couldn't…" voice. Verified — the full
copy is actually fine: title + reassurance ("Your data is safe") +
clear next action. Title is conventional for an error boundary.

High-severity
1. Cash-on-hand input had no accessible label.
   WorkspaceDashboard.vue had a <label> as a sibling of the <input>
   with no for/id binding — screen readers heard an unlabeled number
   input. Added id/for association plus aria-label derived from the
   workspace currency.
2. Unsaved-changes warning on TransactionEditModal close.
   Backdrop click, Escape, X, and Cancel-edit all silently discarded
   edits. Added dirty-state tracking (compares local fields against
   the original transaction) and a ConfirmDialog that fires before
   tear-down when dirty. Two discard targets: 'close' (exit modal)
   and 'view' (revert to read-only without closing).

Medium-severity
3. Period selector converted to WAI-ARIA radiogroup.
   WorkspaceForm.vue Monthly/Custom-dates toggle was plain buttons
   with no arrow-key support and no group semantics. Now role=radiogroup
   + role=radio + aria-checked + roving tabindex; Left/Right/Up/Down
   keys select and move focus to the newly selected option.
4. inputmode="decimal" on all currency number inputs.
   Triggers the iOS numeric-with-decimal keypad instead of the
   telephony keypad. Applied to WorkspaceDashboard cash-on-hand and
   TransactionEditModal amount.
5. Date-format dropdown label now associated via for/id.
   ImportStepUpload.vue had a bare text label sibling to the <select>.
6. Search input label in ImportStepReview.
   Was placeholder-only — not a label per WCAG. Added aria-label and
   changed input type to "search" for the native clear button.

Low-severity
7. File input accessible label in ImportStepUpload.
   Was `class="hidden"` (also removes from tab order) with no aria-label.
   Switched to `sr-only` so keyboard users can Tab to it, added an
   explicit aria-label, and gave the drop-zone label a focus-within
   ring so focus is visible.
8. Tag-input combobox in ImportStepReview now has aria-controls and
   aria-activedescendant — matches the wiring TransactionEditModal got
   in the previous a11y pass. Each list/option gets a stable id per
   transaction index.
9. Cash-on-hand step fixed 100 → 0.01.
   Old step forced values to multiples of 100 currency units; typing
   more granular amounts got coerced.

Type-check clean. 116/116 tests pass. Production build succeeds.

UI verification note: The radiogroup behaviour, unsaved-changes
confirm, and iOS inputmode need manual verification. Focus-change
on arrow-key selection is the subtle one — tab into "Monthly",
press Right, focus should jump to "Custom dates" with it selected.

commits.subject: fix(motion): ApexCharts reduced-motion + bounded kebab pulse
commits.body:
High-severity
1. ApexCharts now respects prefers-reduced-motion.
   The CSS reduced-motion clamp in index.css covers Tailwind/DaisyUI
   animations, but ApexCharts drives SVG animations imperatively via
   its own RAF loop — line draw-in, tooltip fades, and legend
   transitions still played for motion-sensitive users. CashflowGraph
   reads the media query into a reactive ref (with a change listener
   so mid-session OS toggles apply), then passes it to the chart
   options: `chart.animations.enabled: !reducedMotion.value`.
   Added reducedMotion to the chart :key so the instance re-inits
   on preference change — ApexCharts reads animations config at init
   time, not reactively.

Medium-severity
2. Kebab-menu hint pulse now bounded.
   The UX pass removed the 6s auto-dismiss to make the hint persist
   until tapped, but animate-pulse runs forever by default. WCAG 2.2.2
   requires a stop mechanism for motion lasting >5s. Replaced with
   a new `animate-hint-pulse` class (5 iterations ≈ 10s) defined in
   index.css; the surrounding `ring-2 ring-primary/30` remains
   visible after the animation ends, so the affordance is preserved
   without continuous motion.

Dropped finding: the agent flagged HelpDrawer.vue setTimeout leak,
but that file already clears the timer in onUnmounted — verified
at HelpDrawer.vue:50-52. False positive.

Non-motion findings (chart re-render cost, dashboard computed chain,
BottomSheet per-frame reactivity) deferred to the `perf` trigger.

Type-check clean. 116/116 tests pass. Production build succeeds.

UI verification note: The ApexCharts fix needs testing with a real
prefers-reduced-motion setting. On macOS: System Settings → Accessibility
→ Display → Reduce motion. The chart line should appear instantly on
load instead of drawing in.

commits.subject: fix(mobile): 6 mobile UX fixes from frontend.mobile sweep
commits.body:
High-severity
1. Restore error alert no longer sits behind iOS keyboard.
   AppLayout.vue fixed div was `bottom-20` (80px). On iOS with the
   on-screen keyboard or home indicator, the alert fell into the
   obscured zone. Now `bottom: calc(1.5rem + env(safe-area-inset-bottom))`.

Medium-severity
2. Modal-box scrollability made explicit.
   DaisyUI's `.modal-box` doesn't guarantee overflow-y:auto. On small
   phones, TransactionEditModal in edit mode with many tags + ML
   suggestions could exceed 90vh and trap the user. Added
   `max-h-[90vh] overflow-y-auto` to TransactionEditModal,
   InstallInstructionsModal, TutorialModal, and ConfirmDialog for
   consistency.
3. Body scroll locked while any dialog is open.
   Centralized in useDialogA11y — first push to the dialog stack saves
   current inline styles and sets body/html overflow:hidden plus
   overscroll-behavior:contain; last pop restores them. Eliminates
   ghost-scroll under modals, prevents iOS rubber-band exposing page
   edges, and stops pull-to-refresh firing under overlays.
4. Pull-to-refresh gated behind dialog stack.
   usePullToRefresh imports isDialogOpen() from useDialogA11y and
   bails at touchstart if any dialog is up. A downward drag inside
   a modal or sheet no longer triggers a page refresh instead of
   scrolling the sheet contents.
5. ErrorAlert dismiss button now a 40×40 touch target.
   Was a bare 16px icon with no padding — impossible to tap
   reliably on mobile. Wrapped the X in the project's standard
   40×40 flex container (matches other close buttons).

Low-severity
6. Tag removal X buttons bumped 20×20 → 28×28.
   TransactionEditModal tag chips had 20px close buttons — below
   the 44px recommendation and inconsistent with other close
   buttons. 28×28 keeps chip compactness while improving tappability.
   Focus-visible ring added.

verification on a real mobile device. Open TransactionEditModal,
try to pull down on the backdrop — should not scroll the page or
trigger refresh. Open BottomSheet on workspace detail, same test.
Dismiss alert on iOS with keyboard open — alert should stay visible.

commits.subject: fix(a11y): 10 accessibility fixes from frontend.a11y sweep
commits.body:
Highest-impact item is #1 — a regression I introduced in the prior
ux commit when routing transaction delete through ConfirmDialog.

High-severity
1. Dialog stack in useDialogA11y.
   Two open modals (ConfirmDialog inside TransactionEditModal) each
   registered their own document keydown listener. A single Escape
   fired both handlers, so pressing Escape on the inner confirm also
   closed the outer edit modal — losing the entire edit session.
   Refactored to a module-level stack with one shared keydown
   listener; only the topmost dialog handles Escape/Tab. Focus trap
   also yanks focus back if it leaks out of the top dialog.
2. Validation errors announced to screen readers.
   TransactionEditModal now tags the failing input with aria-invalid,
   points aria-describedby at the error, wraps the error in role="alert"
   for auto-announcement, and focuses the first failing input on save.
   Previously a screen-reader user got no feedback when Save failed.

Medium-severity
3. prefers-reduced-motion global clamp in index.css.
   Tailwind animate-*, DaisyUI spinners, and ApexCharts transitions all
   respect the OS-level preference now (vestibular / motion-sensitive
   users). 0.01ms animation/transition duration with !important.
4. Text contrast bumped from /40 → /60 on real content.
   EmptyState description, TransactionTable empty-state messages,
   TransactionEditModal "No tags" italic, CashflowGraph no-data copy.
   40% opacity failed WCAG AA 4.5:1 on both themes for body text.
5. Desktop transaction rows get role="button" + aria-label.
   Was tabindex="0" only — AT users heard a table cell with no cue
   that Enter opens the edit modal. Mobile cards gained matching
   aria-label for parity.
6. Chart text alternative for screen-reader users.
   CashflowGraph wraps the chart in role="img" with a computed
   aria-label summarising period, cumulative direction, forecast
   endpoint, and runway depletion date. ApexCharts SVG is now
   aria-hidden so AT users hear the summary instead of traversing
   the DOM.

Low-severity
7. Tutorial step dots — 32×32 touch target.
   Was 8×8 px — far under the 44px mobile minimum. Visible dot stays
   8px for design continuity, wrapped in a flex-centred 32×32 button.
   Close button bumped to 40×40 to match TransactionEditModal.
8. Pagination contrast and labels.
   ImportStepReview and TransactionTable pagination: disabled opacity
   bumped 30% → 50% (keeps WCAG 3:1 for non-text UI), aria-label on
   Previous/Next clarifies page context, aria-live="polite" on the
   page indicator so the current page announces on change.
9. Combobox aria-controls + aria-activedescendant.
   Tag-input listbox gains an id; input points at it via aria-controls,
   and aria-activedescendant tracks the highlighted option id. Explicit
   combobox wiring that screen readers don't need to infer.
10. aria-busy on loading containers.
    "Suggesting tags..." and "Parsing…" indicators gain role="status"
    + aria-live="polite" + aria-busy so AT users hear the progress
    state, not silence.

Type-check clean. 116/116 tests pass. Production build succeeds.

UI verification note: these changes affect keyboard, screen reader,
and contrast behaviour that can't be asserted from tests alone. The
modal-stack fix in particular should be exercised manually —
TransactionEditModal open, click Delete transaction, press Escape,
verify only ConfirmDialog closes. Then verify Escape on the edit
modal still closes it.

commits.subject: fix(ux): nine targeted UX improvements from frontend.ux sweep
commits.body:
Findings from the `ux` trigger against the whole codebase. Each item
closes a discoverability, feedback, or destructive-action gap a real
user would hit. No architectural changes; all targeted component edits.

High-severity
1. Empty state on dashboard when no transactions imported.
   WorkspaceDashboard.vue now renders an EmptyState with a primary
   "Import transactions" CTA when transactions.length === 0, rather
   than a blank graph + zero metrics + empty table. Suppressed while
   loading to avoid a flash.
2. Transaction delete promoted to ConfirmDialog (was inline panel).
   TransactionEditModal.vue now uses ConfirmDialog for delete, matching
   the pattern already used for workspace delete. Same alertdialog
   semantics, same button weight — destructive actions now consistent.

Medium-severity
3. CSV parse UI no longer appears frozen on large files.
   ImportStepUpload.vue wraps the sync parseCSV call in
   requestAnimationFrame so the "Parsing…" spinner paints before the
   main thread is grabbed. Spinner visual upgraded from text-only
   "Parsing..." to a proper loading-spinner + reassuring copy.
4. ML retry affordance on dashboard (was only in import review).
   WorkspaceDashboard exposes modelError + retryModel through
   TransactionTable → TransactionEditModal. Modal shows a retry link
   when suggestionsError is set and no suggestions exist. Also fixes
   a latent bug: handleRequestSuggestions now awaits waitForModel()
   before calling suggestTags, so users opening a transaction before
   the model finishes loading actually get suggestions once it does.
5. Restore-from-backup loading state.
   useRestoreWorkspace exposes a restoring ref; ConfirmDialog gains
   a `busy` prop that disables both buttons and swaps the confirm
   label for a spinner + "Working…". Prevents the app-looks-frozen
   window during IDB replace transactions and blocks double-clicks.
6. Kebab-menu hint persists until tapped (was 6s auto-dismiss).
   WorkspaceDetailView.vue: pulse continues until user interacts with
   the button. Previously a user who glanced away during the 6s
   window would never see the hint and could miss Save as PDF /
   Export / Edit entirely. Removes kebabHintTimeout entirely.

Low-severity
7. Wizard error banner clears on navigation and retry.
   NewImportWizard.vue clears error at the top of handleUploadComplete
   (stale banner from prior upload no longer persists) and on the
   ImportStepReview back emit.
8. TransactionTable tablet breakpoint moved sm → md.
   Card layout now extends through tablets (< 768px); table kicks in
   only at laptop widths. Prevents awkward tag-column wrapping on
   640-768px screens.
9. Export toast tells the user where the file went.
   "Workspace exported" → "Workspace exported — check your downloads".

commits.subject: fix(cleanup): HMR-safe cleanup for remaining module-level listeners
commits.body:
Completes the TIMER_LEAKS pattern sweep surfaced by pattern-audit. The
prior commit (cf8c1c9) fixed usePWAUpdate.ts; this covers the other
five module-level listener sites identified in that audit's surface
findings.

useDarkMode.ts (2 listeners)
- window 'storage' for cross-tab sync
- mediaQuery 'change' for OS preference fallback
- Added __darkModeAttached guard + import.meta.hot.dispose() to remove
  both on HMR.

usePWAInstall.ts (4 listeners + 1 timeout)
- window 'beforeinstallprompt' (main capture)
- window 'appinstalled'
- standaloneMediaQuery 'change' (install-via-browser-menu detection)
- window 'beforeinstallprompt' { once: true } (diag timeout clear)
- 5s diagnostic setTimeout
- Extracted all anonymous handlers to named functions
  (handleBeforeInstallPrompt, handleAppInstalled, handleStandaloneChange,
  handleEarlyPromptClearDiag) so removeEventListener can reach them.
- Tracked standaloneMediaQuery and diagTimeout at module scope.
- Added __pwaInstallAttached guard + dispose hook that removes all four
  listeners (including the { once: true } pre-fire case), clears the
  media query subscription, and clears diagTimeout.

debugLog.ts (2 listeners + console patches)
- window 'error' and 'unhandledrejection' already had a guard flag but
  no teardown; patched console.error/warn likewise.
- Without dispose, HMR leaves patched console pointing at the orphaned
  old module's debugLog — new events silently routed to a stale buffer
  with no subscribers.
- Hoisted originalError/originalWarn to module scope for restoration.
- Extracted handleGlobalError/handleUnhandledRejection as named handlers.
- Added dispose hook that restores console originals, removes both
  listeners, and resets all three guard flags (__debugConsolePatched,
  __debugLogListenersAttached, __debugLogReady).

No runtime behavior change in production (import.meta.hot is dev-only).
Eliminates the dev-only listener doubling and stale-closure routing
that would accumulate across HMR cycles.

Type-check clean. All 111 tests pass.

commits.subject: fix(pwa): HMR-safe cleanup for module-level visibilitychange listener
commits.body:
Pattern-audit against glow-props TIMER_LEAKS.md §5 flagged
usePWAUpdate.ts as partial: the module-level
`document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', ...)` had no HMR guard
and no dispose hook, so every Vite hot-reload cycle in dev would
double-subscribe the listener and orphan the periodic SW-check
interval.

Changes:
- Extracted the anonymous visibilitychange handler into a named
  function `onVisibilityChange` so removeEventListener can reach it.
- Added `window.__pwaUpdateAttached` guard flag per glow-props pattern
  to prevent double-subscription on module re-evaluation.
- Added `import.meta.hot.dispose()` hook that removes the listener,
  clears `updateCheckInterval`, and resets the guard flag.

No runtime behavior change in production (HMR hooks are dev-only).
Eliminates dev-only listener doubling and interval leaks.

Type-check passes (vue-tsc -b --noEmit clean).

Related: other composables (useDarkMode, usePWAInstall, debugLog)
also attach module-level listeners without HMR guards — surfaced
for separate follow-up, not included in this commit per scope.

commits.subject: feat(pwa): detect icon-only deploys, strengthen tripwire, dedupe precache
commits.body:
Follow-up on cross-repo cleanup sweep — address every self-review item:

- OS icon cache mitigation: new useIconRefresh composable watches iconsHash
  in version.json and surfaces a reinstall banner when (a) the app is
  running standalone AND (b) the current hash differs from the
  last-acknowledged hash. "Show how" opens InstallInstructionsModal with
  the reinstall collapsible pre-expanded (new expand-reinstall prop);
  "Dismiss" acknowledges the current hash so the banner stops reappearing
  until icons change again. First run after a clean install records the
  current hash as acknowledged so users aren't flagged for icons they've
  never seen another version of.
- Version manifest: versionJsonPlugin now takes iconsHash and emits
  { buildTime, iconsHash } so icon-only deploys become detectable without
  relying on sw.js revision bumps.
- Workbox precache dedup: dropped redundant includeAssets since
  globPatterns already sweeps ico/png/svg from /public. Precache count
  34 → 31 entries — favicon.ico, favicon-48x48.png, apple-touch-icon.png
  no longer listed twice in sw.js.
- Tripwire strengthened (iconCacheBust.test.ts, 6 → 9 tests):
  • Source-level: scans the manifest.icons block and fails on any bare
    string src (earlier version only checked 3 hardcoded paths, so a new
    icon added without versioned() slipped through).
  • Source-level: asserts versionJsonPlugin(ICONS_HASH) wiring.
  • Dist-level: verifies sw.js precache has no duplicate URLs.
  • Dist-level: verifies version.json carries iconsHash.
  Verified by breaking the bare-string guard (test fails) then restoring
  (test passes).
- Save-as-PDF rationale: strengthened handlePrint() comment to explain
  why the per-workspace actions menu beats the burger-menu alternative
  — printing only makes sense on a workspace page, and a global burger
  entry would either print nothing off-workspace or need route-aware
  visibility. CLAUDE.md Project Status updated to match.
- Docs: CLAUDE.md Project Status gained a PWA icon cache-busting bullet;
  README.md tree lists the new composable; SESSION_NOTES rewritten.

All 115 tests pass, vue-tsc clean, npm run build emits versioned hrefs
in dist/index.html, dist/manifest.webmanifest icon srcs, dist/sw.js
precache with zero duplicate URLs, and dist/version.json with iconsHash.

- Content-hash every PWA icon URL: iconCacheBustHtml() Vite plugin rewrites

  same ?v=<sha256-8> into the manifest. Workbox now strips the v param on

  current demand.
- Save-as-PDF button was already in place (WorkspaceDetailView.vue:152);

commits.subject: fix(pwa): Chromium fallback instructions were unreachable
commits.body:
The Chromium fallback instructions added to the install modal could
never be displayed because needsManualInstructions returned false for
Chromium browsers without a native prompt. The modal only opens when
showInstallPrompt is true, which requires either isNativeInstallAvailable
or needsManualInstructions.

Fix: After the 5-second diagnostic timeout fires on a Chromium browser
without a beforeinstallprompt event, set chromiumFallback ref to true.
needsManualInstructions now includes this flag, making the install
banner show "How to Install" with manual steps (address bar icon,
browser menu). This covers the 90-day suppression window after a user
dismisses Chrome's native prompt.

Also added clarifying comment for iOS Firefox → firefox-desktop mapping.

commits.subject: fix(pwa): settle delay, cleanupOutdatedCaches, install prompt diagnostic
commits.body:
Three improvements found during pattern audit:

1. checkForUpdate() now has a 1500ms settle delay after
   registration.update() — SW lifecycle events fire asynchronously
   after update() resolves, so without the delay the "Checking..."
   state flashes invisibly fast and events may not have propagated.
   Also added error handling with debugLog.

2. cleanupOutdatedCaches: true in workbox config — removes stale
   caches from incompatible older Workbox major versions that
   accumulate across deployments.

3. Fixed DebugPill install prompt diagnostic: usePWAInstall deletes
   window.__pwaInstallPromptEvent after consuming it, so the
   diagnostic always showed "Not received." Now sets a
   __pwaInstallPromptReceived flag before deleting, and DebugPill
   checks both sources.

commits.subject: feat(pwa): visibility-based update checks + 30s post-update suppression
commits.body:
Two improvements to service worker update detection from glow-props
PWA_SYSTEM pattern:

1. visibilitychange listener triggers registration.update() when the
   tab regains focus — catches deploys that happened while the tab was
   backgrounded, without waiting for the next 60-minute interval.

2. wasJustUpdated() checks a sessionStorage timestamp to suppress false
   update re-detection within 30 seconds of applying an update. The
   browser's SW lifecycle may fire statechange events during activation
   that look like a new update is available.

PWA diagnostics tab (task 3) was already implemented in the prior
DEBUG_SYSTEM sync — no changes needed.

commits.subject: fix: harden edge cases from codebase audit
commits.body:
1. Import wizard state reset: Clear parsedRows, classifiedGroups, step,
   and error on mount — prevents stale data leaking into second import.

2. CSV parser unclosed quotes: parseCSVRow and splitCSVLines now treat
   unclosed quotes as end-of-field rather than corrupting all subsequent
   field parsing for the rest of the file.

3. Cluster memberIndices bounds check: Filter out-of-bounds indices before
   accessing groups array during embedding-based merge — prevents crash
   if clustering returns unexpected indices.

4. Date disambiguation validation: Skip malformed date samples (missing
   segments, NaN values) instead of crashing on parts[0]/parts[1] access.

5. Forecast prediction bands capped at 90 days: Beyond that, sqrt(horizon)
   scaling makes bands too wide to be actionable. Bands now plateau at
   day 90 rather than growing indefinitely.

(MoritzLaurer/xtremedistil-l6-h256-zeroshot-v1.1-all-33, ~13MB quantized).

composable (useTagSuggestions) manages lazy model loading, caching (via
browser Cache API), and inference. Graceful degradation — no tags in cache
means no model loaded, no suggestions shown, zero impact on existing flow.

commits.subject: feat(engines): type-aware actuals splitting across all engines
commits.body:

expense type (actual.expenseId → expense.type) instead of blindly
treating all actuals as expenses. This prevents salary deposits from
inflating "actual spend" in cashflow, envelope, and variance engines.

  independently uses actuals where available
- Envelope: filters to expense-type actuals only for burn rate/depletion

- Matching: originalSign on ImportedRow, isTypeCompatible() prefers
  same-type matches (negative CSV amounts → income), fallback to any
- ImportStepMapping: preserves originalSign before Math.abs()
- Views: CashflowTab/CompareTab pass expenses to engines
